scene.description: A version of you that an earlier choice suppressed past recovery. Almost nothing survives — a fragment, decaying as you watch. This is what 'erasing the others' actually looks like from the inside.

fragments.label: What's Left of a Face
fragments.symbol: A face, mostly gone, the way the lighthouse subject's was — except this one was yours. It is being unrendered in real time and there is nothing to examine before it finishes.

fragments.label: The Choice That Did This
fragments.text: You can feel which selection killed this version. It was a reasonable choice. It was maybe even the right one. It still did this.

fragments.label: The Last Intact Word
fragments.text: One word survives, the last thing this self managed to think before suppression: not a name, not a plea. Just: 'again.'

interpretations.label: Mourn this lost self — mark that it existed
Let it finish decaying — it's already gone
